verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a type 1 transmembrane protein resident in the ER membrane. Under basal conditions it resides predominantly in the ER lumen-facing domain. However, upon Ca²⁺ store depletion, STIM1 undergoes a well-characterized conformational change, oligomerizes, and translocates to ER-PM junctional regions where it directly interacts with and gates Orai1 Ca²⁺ channels on the plasma membrane. During this activated state, STIM1's C-terminal cytoplasmic domain reaches the outer face of the PM through stable junctional clustering with its TM partner Orai1. This represents a cell-state-induced (stimulus-dependent) surface accessibility mechanism triggered by ER Ca²⁺ depletion.
